parentQuestion: The type of cartilage found in the external ear is ___.

scaffolds.q: The external ear can be bent and folded, then springs back to its original shape.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Try it - bend your ear. It returns to its original shape. This requires flexibility WITH shape memory.

scaffolds.q: There are three types of cartilage: hyaline, elastic, and fibrocartilage.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Each type has different mechanical properties suited for different body locations.

scaffolds.q: ELASTIN fibers provide stretch and recoil - like a rubber band.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Elastin can stretch and snap back. Collagen resists stretching. Different jobs, different fibers.

scaffolds.q: ELASTIC cartilage contains elastin fibers that give it flexibility.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The elastin fibers let elastic cartilage bend and spring back to shape.

scaffolds.q: Hyaline cartilage is smooth and found on joint surfaces - not as flexible as elastic cartilage.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Hyaline is firm and smooth for gliding movements in joints. Elastic cartilage is for bending.

scaffolds.q: Fibrocartilage is tough and found in intervertebral discs - not flexible like the ear.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Fibrocartilage handles compression forces. The ear needs flexibility, not shock absorption.

scaffolds.q: Elastic cartilage is found in the ear, epiglottis, and larynx - places needing flexibility.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The epiglottis flaps over the airway, the ear bends, the larynx changes shape for voice. All need elastic cartilage.

scaffolds.q: The external ear is made of ELASTIC cartilage because it needs to bend and return to shape.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The elastin fibers give it that spring-back property you feel when you bend your ear.

scaffolds.q: The answer is ELASTIC cartilage - flexible with shape retention, perfect for the ear.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Not hyaline (for smooth gliding), not fibrocartilage (for compression). Elastic = flexibility + shape memory.
